task_goal: Extract atomic Events and Occasions first, and also extract their explicitly linked characters, times, locations, and other related entities so later stages can build relations and ground entities automatically.
global_constraints: Use only information grounded in the text; do not invent content.
Keep Events atomic; do not collapse multiple distinct actions into one Event.
An Occasion is an organized activity or social container, not a single action.
Keep names close to the surface form and original language of the text; do not translate without reason.
Attached entities should include only name and description; do not assign schema types yet.
extraction_rules: If the same passage supports both an Occasion and one or more Events inside it, extract both.
For each Event, include agents / experiencers / patients / locations / times / occasion / related_entities whenever they are clearly supported.
For each Occasion, include participants / initiators / locations / times / related_entities whenever they are clearly supported.
If a character or entity is only weak background mention, do not force it onto every frame.
Keep related_entities only when they have standalone node value; do not output propositional items such as a piece of news, a claim, or the content of one utterance.

events.name: Doctor informs the family of the critical condition
events.description: A doctor explains outside the ward that the patient's condition has worsened.

agents.name: doctor
agents.description: The person explaining the medical situation

patients.name: family members
patients.description: The people receiving the bad news

locations.name: hospital corridor outside the ward
locations.description: The corridor outside the patient's room

times.name: late night
times.description: The time period when the event happens

related_entities.name: critical condition notice
related_entities.description: A document used to communicate the worsening condition
